html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume One — Part Four

The Torah — First Book

BERESHIT

In the Beginning

Chapters 37 through 50

Yosef — Yaakov Descends to Egypt — The Deathbed Blessings

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

4QGena (4Q1) · 4QGenb (4Q2) · 4QGenc (4Q3) · 4QGend (4Q4)

4QGene (4Q5) · 4QGenf (4Q6) · 4QGenh (4Q8) · 4QGenj (4Q10) · 4QGenk (4Q11)

1QapGen — Genesis Apocryphon — Cave 1 — Aramaic

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Beta Israel Ancient Canon

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitutions

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

THE STORY OF YOSEF — CHAPTERS 37 THROUGH 45

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 37 Yosef's Dreams — Sold into Egypt

37:1 Yaakov lived in the land of his father's sojournings, in the land of Kena'an.

37:2 These are the generations of Yaakov. Yosef, being seventeen years old, was pasturing the flock with his brothers. He was a boy with the sons of Bilhah and Zilpah, his father's wives. And Yosef brought a bad report of them to their father.

37:3 Now Yisra'EL loved Yosef more than any other of his sons, because he was the son of his old age. And he made him a robe of many colors.

37:4 But when his brothers saw that their father loved him more than all his brothers, they hated him and could not speak peacefully to him.

37:5 Now Yosef had a dream, and when he told it to his brothers they hated him even more.

37:6 He said to them: Hear this dream that I have dreamed.

37:7 Behold, we were binding sheaves in the field, and behold, my sheaf arose and stood upright. And behold, your sheaves gathered around it and bowed down to my sheaf.

37:8 His brothers said to him: Are you indeed to reign over us? Or are you indeed to rule over us? So they hated him even more for his dreams and for his words.

37:9 Then he dreamed another dream and told it to his brothers and said: Behold, the sun, the moon, and eleven stars were bowing down to me.

37:10 But when he told it to his father and to his brothers, his father rebuked him and said to him: What is this dream that you have dreamed? Shall I and your mother and your brothers indeed come to bow ourselves to the ground before you?

37:11 And his brothers were jealous of him, but his father kept the saying in mind.

37:13 And Yisra'EL said to Yosef: Are not your brothers pasturing the flock at Shechem? Come, I will send you to them. And he said to him: Here I am.

37:14 He said to him: Go now, see if it is well with your brothers and with the flock, and bring me word. So he sent him from the Valley of Hebron, and he came to Shechem.

37:17 And Yosef went after his brothers and found them at Dotan.

37:18 They saw him from afar, and before he came near to them they conspired against him to kill him.

37:19 They said to one another: Here comes this dreamer.

37:20 Come now, let us kill him and throw him into one of the pits. Then we will say that a fierce animal has devoured him, and we will see what will become of his dreams.

37:21 But when Re'uven heard it, he rescued him out of their hands, saying: Let us not take his life.

37:22 And Re'uven said to them: Shed no blood — throw him into this pit here in the wilderness, but do not lay a hand on him — that he might rescue him out of their hand to restore him to his father.

37:23 So when Yosef came to his brothers, they stripped him of his robe, the robe of many colors that he wore.

37:24 And they took him and threw him into a pit. The pit was empty — there was no water in it.

37:25 Then they sat down to eat. And looking up they saw a caravan of Yishma'elites coming from Gilead, with their camels bearing gum, balm, and myrrh, on their way to carry it down to Egypt.

37:26 Then Yehudah said to his brothers: What profit is it if we kill our brother and conceal his blood?

37:27 Come, let us sell him to the Yishma'elites, and let not our hand be upon him, for he is our brother, our own flesh. And his brothers listened to him.

37:28 Then Midyanite traders passed by. And they drew Yosef up and lifted him out of the pit, and sold him to the Yishma'elites for twenty pieces of silver. They took Yosef to Egypt.

37:29 When Re'uven returned to the pit and saw that Yosef was not in the pit, he tore his clothes

37:30 And returned to his brothers and said: The boy is gone, and I, where shall I go?

37:31 Then they took Yosef's robe and slaughtered a goat and dipped the robe in the blood.

37:32 And they sent the robe of many colors and brought it to their father and said: This we have found — please identify whether it is your son's robe or not.

37:33 And he identified it and said: It is my son's robe. A fierce animal has devoured him. Yosef is without doubt torn to pieces.

37:34 Then Yaakov tore his garments and put sackcloth on his loins and mourned for his son many days.

37:35 All his sons and all his daughters rose up to comfort him, but he refused to be comforted and said: No, I shall go down to Sheol to my son, mourning. Thus his father wept for him.

37:36 Meanwhile the Midyanites had sold him in Egypt to Potifar, an officer of Pharaoh, the captain of the guard.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena and 4QGenb preserve portions of Chapter 37. The sale of Yosef and the deception of Yaakov are attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 37 in full. Yovelim 34:1-19 covers the sale of Yosef and notes YHWH was with Yosef despite what was done to him.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 37:11 — his father kept the saying in mind. Yaakov rebuked Yosef publicly but stored the dream. He had his own encounter with YHWH in dreams. He recognized what a significant dream looked like and could not dismiss what he heard.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 37:26-27 — Yehudah saves Yosef from death but consigns him to slavery. In Chapter 44 Yehudah will offer himself as a slave to save Binyamin. That arc — from the man who sold his brother to the man who offers himself for his brother — is Yehudah's complete moral transformation. The covenant passes through Yehudah not because he started righteous but because he became so.

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 38 Yehudah and Tamar

38:1 It happened at that time that Yehudah went down from his brothers and turned aside to a certain Adullamite, whose name was Hirah.

38:2 There Yehudah saw the daughter of a certain Kena'anite whose name was Shua. He took her and went in to her,

38:3 And she conceived and bore a son, and he called his name Er.

38:4 She conceived again and bore a son, and she called his name Onan.

38:5 Yet again she bore a son, and she called his name Shelah.

38:6 And Yehudah took a wife for Er his firstborn, and her name was Tamar.

38:7 But Er, Yehudah's firstborn, was wicked in the sight of YHWH, and YHWH put him to death.

38:8 Then Yehudah said to Onan: Go in to your brother's wife and perform the duty of a brother-in-law to her, and raise up offspring for your brother.

38:9 But Onan knew that the offspring would not be his. So whenever he went in to his brother's wife he would waste the semen on the ground, so as not to give offspring to his brother.

38:10 And what he did was wicked in the sight of YHWH, and he put him to death also.

38:11 Then Yehudah said to Tamar his daughter-in-law: Remain a widow in your father's house, till Shelah my son grows up. So Tamar went and remained in her father's house.

38:14 She took off her widow's garments and covered herself with a veil, wrapping herself up, and sat at the entrance to Enaim, which is on the road to Timnah. For she saw that Shelah was grown up, and she had not been given to him in marriage.

38:16 He turned to her at the roadside and said: Come, let me come in to you — for he did not know that she was his daughter-in-law. She said: What will you give me, that you may come in to me?

38:18 He said: What pledge shall I give you? She replied: Your signet and your cord and your staff. So he gave them to her and went in to her, and she conceived by him.

38:24 About three months later Yehudah was told: Tamar your daughter-in-law has been immoral — she is pregnant by immorality. And Yehudah said: Bring her out, and let her be burned.

38:25 As she was being brought out, she sent word to her father-in-law: By the man to whom these belong, I am pregnant. Please identify whose these are, the signet and the cord and the staff.

38:26 Then Yehudah identified them and said: She is more righteous than I, since I did not give her to my son Shelah. And he did not know her again.

38:29 And afterward his brother came out with the scarlet thread on his hand, and he was called Zerah.

38:30 And afterward his brother came out, and he was called Perets.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena preserves portions of Chapter 38.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 38 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 38:26 — she is more righteous than I. Yehudah had the power to have Tamar executed. He chose instead to confess his own failure publicly. She acted to preserve the covenant line he had neglected. Perets, born from this union, carries the lineage forward. The covenant runs through a union that began in deception on both sides — and YHWH was in it.

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 39 Yosef in Potifar's House — Falsely Accused — Cast into Prison

39:1 Now Yosef had been brought down to Egypt, and Potifar, an officer of Pharaoh, the captain of the guard, an Egyptian, had bought him from the Yishma'elites who had brought him down there.

39:2 YHWH was with Yosef, and he became a successful man, and he was in the house of his Egyptian master.

39:3 His master saw that YHWH was with him and that YHWH caused all that he did to succeed in his hands.

39:4 So Yosef found favor in his sight and attended him, and he made him overseer of his house and put him in charge of all that he had.

39:5 From the time that he made him overseer in his house YHWH blessed the Egyptian's house for Yosef's sake. The blessing of YHWH was on all that he had, in house and field.

39:6 So he left all that he had in Yosef's charge. Now Yosef was handsome in form and appearance.

39:7 And after a time his master's wife cast her eyes on Yosef and said: Lie with me.

39:8 But he refused and said to his master's wife: Behold, because of me my master has no concern about anything in the house, and he has put everything that he has in my charge.

39:9 He is not greater in this house than I am, nor has he kept back anything from me except you, because you are his wife. How then can I do this great wickedness and sin against The Creator?

39:10 And as she spoke to Yosef day after day, he would not listen to her, to lie beside her or to be with her.

39:12 She caught him by his garment, saying: Lie with me. But he left his garment in her hand and fled and got out of the house.

39:20 And Yosef's master took him and put him into the prison where the king's prisoners were confined, and he was there in prison.

39:21 But YHWH was with Yosef and showed him steadfast love and gave him favor in the sight of the keeper of the prison.

39:22 And the keeper of the prison put Yosef in charge of all the prisoners who were in the prison. Whatever was done there, he was the one who did it.

39:23 The keeper of the prison paid no attention to anything that was in Yosef's charge, because YHWH was with him. And whatever he did, YHWH made it succeed.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena and 4QGenb preserve portions of Chapter 39. YHWH's presence with Yosef in Potifar's house and in prison is attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 39 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 39:9 — how can I do this great wickedness and sin against The Creator? Yosef's refusal is stated in theological terms — not loyalty to Potifar, not fear of consequences, but refusal to sin against The Creator. Ethical behavior in the ancient Hebrew framework is first covenantal. Sin is against YHWH before it is against any man.

[CRITICAL NOTE] YHWH was with Yosef appears three times in this chapter alone. He is a slave in Potifar's house — YHWH is with him. He is in prison on a false accusation — YHWH is with him. The presence of YHWH is not contingent on circumstances and is not withdrawn by injustice. This is the theological spine of the entire Yosef narrative.

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 40 The Dreams of the Cupbearer and the Baker

40:1 Some time after this, the cupbearer of the king of Egypt and his baker committed an offense against their lord the king of Egypt.

40:3 And he put them in custody in the house of the captain of the guard, in the prison where Yosef was confined.

40:4 The captain of the guard appointed Yosef to be with them, and he attended them.

40:5 And one night they both dreamed — the cupbearer and the baker of the king of Egypt, who were confined in the prison — each his own dream, and each dream with its own interpretation.

40:6 When Yosef came to them in the morning, he saw that they were troubled.

40:8 They said to him: We have had dreams, and there is no one to interpret them. And Yosef said to them: Do not interpretations belong to The Creator? Please tell them to me.

40:9 So the chief cupbearer told his dream to Yosef and said to him: In my dream there was a vine before me,

40:10 And on the vine there were three branches. As soon as it budded, its blossoms shot forth, and the clusters ripened into grapes.

40:11 Pharaoh's cup was in my hand, and I took the grapes and pressed them into Pharaoh's cup and placed the cup in Pharaoh's hand.

40:12 Then Yosef said to him: This is its interpretation — the three branches are three days.

40:13 In three days Pharaoh will lift up your head and restore you to your office, and you shall place Pharaoh's cup in his hand as formerly, when you were his cupbearer.

40:14 Only remember me, when it is well with you, and please do me the kindness to mention me to Pharaoh, and so get me out of this house.

40:15 For I was indeed stolen out of the land of the Hebrews, and here also I have done nothing that they should put me into the pit.

40:16 When the chief baker saw that the interpretation was favorable, he said to Yosef: I also had a dream — there were three cake baskets on my head.

40:18 And Yosef answered and said: This is its interpretation — the three baskets are three days.

40:19 In three days Pharaoh will lift up your head — from you — and hang you on a tree. And the birds will eat the flesh from you.

40:20 On the third day, which was Pharaoh's birthday, he made a feast for all his servants and lifted up the head of the chief cupbearer and the head of the chief baker among his servants.

40:21 He restored the chief cupbearer to his position, and he placed the cup in Pharaoh's hand.

40:22 But he hanged the chief baker, as Yosef had interpreted to them.

40:23 Yet the chief cupbearer did not remember Yosef, but forgot him.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena preserves portions of Chapter 40.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 40 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 40:8 — do not interpretations belong to The Creator? Yosef presents interpretation as a gift from The Creator, not a personal skill. This positioning — the ability comes from YHWH, not from Yosef — is repeated before Pharaoh in Chapter 41. He never claims the power. He channels the source.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 40:23 — yet the chief cupbearer did not remember Yosef but forgot him. Two more years of imprisonment follow. YHWH's timing, not human gratitude, determines when Yosef rises. The forgetting is part of the design.

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 41 Pharaoh's Dreams — Yosef Raised to Second in Egypt

41:1 After two whole years, Pharaoh dreamed that he was standing by the Nile,

41:2 And behold, there came up out of the Nile seven cows, attractive and plump, and they fed in the reed grass.

41:3 And behold, seven other cows, ugly and thin, came up out of the Nile after them, and stood by the other cows on the bank of the Nile.

41:4 And the ugly, thin cows ate up the seven attractive, plump cows. And Pharaoh awoke.

41:5 And he fell asleep and dreamed a second time. And behold, seven ears of grain, plump and good, were growing on one stalk.

41:6 And behold, after them sprouted seven ears, thin and blighted by the east wind.

41:7 And the thin ears swallowed up the seven plump, full ears. And Pharaoh awoke, and behold, it was a dream.

41:8 So in the morning his spirit was troubled, and he sent and called for all the magicians of Egypt and all its wise men. Pharaoh told them his dreams, but there was none who could interpret them to Pharaoh.

41:9 Then the chief cupbearer said to Pharaoh: I remember my offenses today.

41:14 Then Pharaoh sent and called Yosef, and they quickly brought him out of the pit. And when he had shaved himself and changed his clothes, he came in before Pharaoh.

41:15 And Pharaoh said to Yosef: I have had a dream, and there is no one who can interpret it. I have heard it said of you that when you hear a dream you can interpret it.

41:16 Yosef answered Pharaoh: It is not in me — The Creator will give Pharaoh a favorable answer.

41:25 Then Yosef said to Pharaoh: The dreams of Pharaoh are one — The Creator has revealed to Pharaoh what he is about to do.

41:26 The seven good cows are seven years, and the seven good ears are seven years — the dreams are one.

41:27 The seven lean and ugly cows that came up after them are seven years, and the seven empty ears blighted by the east wind are also seven years of famine.

41:29 There will come seven years of great plenty throughout all the land of Egypt,

41:30 But after them there will arise seven years of famine, and all the plenty will be forgotten in the land of Egypt. The famine will consume the land.

41:32 And the doubling of Pharaoh's dream means that the thing is fixed by The Creator, and The Creator will shortly bring it about.

41:33 Now therefore let Pharaoh select a discerning and wise man, and set him over the land of Egypt.

41:37 This proposal pleased Pharaoh and all his servants.

41:38 And Pharaoh said to his servants: Can we find a man like this, in whom is the Spirit of The Creator?

41:39 Then Pharaoh said to Yosef: Since The Creator has shown you all this, there is none so discerning and wise as you are.

41:40 You shall be over my house, and all my people shall order themselves as you command. Only as regards the throne will I be greater than you.

41:41 And Pharaoh said to Yosef: See, I have set you over all the land of Egypt.

41:42 Then Pharaoh took his signet ring from his hand and put it on Yosef's hand, and clothed him in garments of fine linen and put a gold chain about his neck.

41:43 And he made him ride in his second chariot. And they called out before him: Bow the knee. Thus he set him over all the land of Egypt.

41:45 And Pharaoh called Yosef's name Tzafnat-Paneach. And he gave him in marriage Asenath, the daughter of Poti-Fera priest of On. So Yosef went out over the land of Egypt.

41:46 Yosef was thirty years old when he entered the service of Pharaoh king of Egypt. And Yosef went out from the presence of Pharaoh and went through all the land of Egypt.

41:50 Before the year of famine came, two sons were born to Yosef. Asenath, the daughter of Poti-Fera priest of On, bore them to him.

41:51 Yosef called the name of the firstborn Manasseh. For he said: The Creator has made me forget all my hardship and all my father's house.

41:52 The name of the second he called Efrayim, for The Creator has made me fruitful in the land of my affliction.

41:53 The seven years of plenty that occurred in the land of Egypt came to an end,

41:54 And the seven years of famine began to come, as Yosef had said. There was famine in all lands, but in all the land of Egypt there was bread.

41:56 So when the famine had spread over all the land, Yosef opened all the storehouses and sold to the Egyptians, for the famine was severe in the land of Egypt.

41:57 Moreover, all the earth came to Egypt to Yosef to buy grain, because the famine was severe over all the earth.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena and 4QGenb preserve portions of Chapter 41. Yosef's elevation by Pharaoh is attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 41 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 41:16 — it is not in me — The Creator will give Pharaoh a favorable answer. Yosef stands before the most powerful man in the world, thirteen years after being thrown into a pit by his own brothers, and his first words disclaim any personal power. This is the theological character of Yosef throughout. Power flows through him. It does not originate in him.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 41:38 — can we find a man like this, in whom is the Spirit of The Creator? Pharaoh — an Egyptian ruler — recognizes the Spirit of The Creator in Yosef before any Yisra'ELi king or prophet makes a similar declaration. The ancient record does not restrict divine recognition to the covenant community. YHWH's Spirit is visible to Pharaoh. The Creator's work in Yosef is legible to the nations.

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 42 The Brothers Come to Egypt — Yosef Tests Them

42:1 When Yaakov learned that there was grain for sale in Egypt, he said to his sons: Why do you look at one another?

42:2 And he said: Behold, I have heard that there is grain for sale in Egypt. Go down and buy grain for us there, that we may live and not die.

42:3 So ten of Yosef's brothers went down to buy grain in Egypt.

42:4 But Yaakov did not send Binyamin, Yosef's brother, with his brothers, for he feared that harm might happen to him.

42:5 Thus the sons of Yisra'EL came to buy among the others who came, for the famine was in the land of Kena'an.

42:6 Now Yosef was governor over the land. He was the one who sold to all the people of the land. And Yosef's brothers came and bowed themselves before him with their faces to the ground.

42:7 Yosef saw his brothers and recognized them, but he treated them like strangers and spoke roughly to them. He said to them: Where do you come from? They said: From the land of Kena'an, to buy food.

42:8 And Yosef recognized his brothers, but they did not recognize him.

42:9 And Yosef remembered the dreams that he had dreamed of them. And he said to them: You are spies — you have come to see the nakedness of the land.

42:11 We are all sons of one man. We are honest men. Your servants have never been spies.

42:13 And they said: We, your servants, are twelve brothers, the sons of one man in the land of Kena'an. And behold, the youngest is this day with our father, and one is no more.

42:15 By this you shall be tested — by the life of Pharaoh, you shall not go from this place unless your youngest brother comes here.

42:16 Send one of you, and let him bring your brother, while you remain confined, that your words may be tested, whether there is truth in you. Or else, by the life of Pharaoh, surely you are spies.

42:17 And he put them all together in custody for three days.

42:21 Then they said to one another: In truth we are guilty concerning our brother, in that we saw the distress of his soul, when he begged us and we did not listen. That is why this distress has come upon us.

42:22 And Re'uven answered them: Did I not tell you not to sin against the boy? But you did not listen. So now there comes a reckoning for his blood.

42:23 They did not know that Yosef understood them, for there was an interpreter between them.

42:24 Then he turned away from them and wept. And he returned to them and spoke to them. And he took Shim'on from them and bound him before their eyes.

42:25 And Yosef gave orders to fill their bags with grain, and to replace every man's money in his sack, and to give them provisions for the journey. This was done for them.

42:28 He said to his brothers: My money has been put back — here it is in my sack. At this their hearts failed them, and they turned trembling to one another, saying: What is this that The Creator has done to us?

42:36 And their father Yaakov said to them: You have bereaved me of my children. Yosef is no more, and Shim'on is no more, and now you would take Binyamin. All this has come against me.

42:38 But he said: My son shall not go down with you, for his brother is dead, and he is the only one left. If harm should happen to him on the journey that you are to make, you would bring down my gray hairs with sorrow to Sheol.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena and 4QGenb preserve portions of Chapter 42. The brothers coming before Yosef and his recognition of them is attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 42 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 42:21 — in truth we are guilty concerning our brother. Twenty years after the pit at Dotan the brothers speak the truth to one another for the first time. Distress in Egypt unlocks the confession they could never make in Kena'an. YHWH uses the pressure of circumstances to surface what was buried. The reckoning they feared has arrived — not as punishment from Yosef, who does not yet reveal himself, but as conscience speaking at last.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 42:24 — then he turned away from them and wept. Yosef weeps multiple times throughout these chapters. He is the most emotionally present figure in all of Bereshit. He wept when he heard his brothers confessing guilt. He wept when he saw Binyamin. He wept when he revealed himself. He wept on his father's neck. The ancient record does not edit out the tears of the patriarch.

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 43 The Brothers Return with Binyamin

43:1 Now the famine was severe in the land.

43:2 And when they had eaten the grain that they had brought from Egypt, their father said to them: Go again, buy us a little food.

43:3 But Yehudah said to him: The man solemnly warned us, saying: You shall not see my face unless your brother is with you.

43:4 If you will send our brother with us, we will go down and buy you food.

43:5 But if you will not send him, we will not go down, for the man said to us: You shall not see my face, unless your brother is with you.

43:8 And Yehudah said to Yisra'EL his father: Send the boy with me, and we will arise and go, that we may live and not die, both we and you and also our little ones.

43:9 I will be a pledge of his safety. From my hand you shall require him. If I do not bring him back to you and set him before you, then let me bear the blame forever.

43:11 Then their father Yisra'EL said to them: If it must be so, then do this — take some of the choice fruits of the land in your bags, and carry a present down to the man, a little balm and a little honey, gum, myrrh, pistachio nuts, and almonds.

43:14 May The Creator Almighty grant you mercy before the man, and may he send back your other brother and Binyamin. And as for me, if I am bereaved of my children, I am bereaved.

43:16 When Yosef saw Binyamin with them, he said to the steward of his house: Bring the men into the house, and slaughter an animal and make ready, for the men are to dine with me at noon.

43:26 When Yosef came home, they brought into the house to him the present that they had with them and bowed down to him to the ground.

43:27 And he inquired about their welfare and said: Is your father well, the old man of whom you spoke? Is he still alive?

43:28 They said: Your servant our father is well — he is still alive. And they bowed their heads and prostrated themselves.

43:29 And he lifted up his eyes and saw his brother Binyamin, his mother's son, and said: Is this your youngest brother, of whom you spoke to me? The Creator be gracious to you, my son.

43:30 Then Yosef hurried out, for his compassion grew warm for his brother, and he sought a place to weep. And he entered his chamber and wept there.

43:34 Portions were taken to them from Yosef's table, but Binyamin's portion was five times as much as any of theirs. And they drank and were merry with him.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena preserves portions of Chapter 43.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 43 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 43:9 — I will be a pledge of his safety. Yehudah offers himself as surety for Binyamin before his father. This is not the Yehudah of Chapter 37 who proposed selling his brother for silver. Something has broken and reformed in him. He stands before his father and takes personal responsibility for the life of another. The covenant line running through Yehudah is anchored in this transformed character.

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 44 The Silver Cup — Yehudah's Plea

44:1 Then he commanded the steward of his house: Fill the men's sacks with food, as much as they can carry, and put each man's money in the mouth of his sack,

44:2 And put my cup, the silver cup, in the mouth of the sack of the youngest, with his money for the grain. And he did as Yosef told him.

44:4 When they had gone out of the city, and were not yet far off, Yosef said to his steward: Up, follow after the men, and when you overtake them, say to them: Why have you repaid evil for good?

44:6 He overtook them, and he spoke to them these words.

44:7 They said to him: Why does my lord speak such words as these? Far be it from your servants to do such a thing.

44:9 Whichever of your servants is found with it shall die, and we also will be my lord's servants.

44:12 He searched, beginning with the eldest and ending with the youngest. And the cup was found in Binyamin's sack.

44:13 Then they tore their garments, and every man loaded his donkey, and they returned to the city.

44:14 When Yehudah and his brothers came to Yosef's house, he was still there. They fell before him to the ground.

44:16 And Yehudah said: What shall we say to my lord? What shall we speak? Or how can we clear ourselves? The Creator has found out the guilt of your servants — behold, we are my lord's servants, both we and he also in whose hand the cup has been found.

44:17 But he said: Far be it from me that I should do so. Only the man in whose hand the cup was found shall be my servant. But as for you, go up in peace to your father.

44:18 Then Yehudah went up to him and said: O my lord, please let your servant speak a word in my lord's ears, and let not your anger burn against your servant, for you are like Pharaoh himself.

44:20 And we said to my lord: We have a father, an old man, and a young brother, the child of his old age. His brother is dead, and he alone is left of his mother's children, and his father loves him.

44:22 We said to my lord: The boy cannot leave his father, for if he should leave his father, his father would die.

44:27 And your servant my father said to us: You know that my wife bore me two sons.

44:28 One left me, and I said: Surely he has been torn to pieces, and I have never seen him since.

44:29 If you take this one also from me, and harm happens to him, you will bring down my gray hairs in evil to Sheol.

44:30 Now therefore, as soon as I come to your servant my father, and the boy is not with us, then, as his life is bound up in the boy's life,

44:31 As soon as he sees that the boy is not with us, he will die, and your servants will bring down the gray hairs of your servant our father with sorrow to Sheol.

44:32 For your servant became a pledge of safety for the boy to my father, saying: If I do not bring him back to you, then I shall bear the blame before my father all my life.

44:33 Now therefore, please let your servant remain instead of the boy as a servant to my lord, and let the boy go back with his brothers.

44:34 For how can I go back to my father if the boy is not with me? I fear to see the evil that would find my father.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena and 4QGenb preserve portions of Chapter 44. Yehudah's plea before Yosef is attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 44 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 44:33 — please let your servant remain instead of the boy as a servant to my lord. This is the moment of Yehudah's complete transformation. The man who sold Yosef for silver now offers himself as a slave to set his brother free. He does not offer a defense. He does not negotiate. He offers himself. This is what breaks Yosef open in the very next verse of Chapter 45. The ancient record understands that genuine repentance is not confession of guilt alone — it is the willingness to bear the cost of another.

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 45 Yosef Reveals Himself — Yaakov Called to Egypt

45:1 Then Yosef could not control himself before all those who stood by him. He cried: Make everyone go out from me. So no one stayed with him when Yosef made himself known to his brothers.

45:2 And he wept aloud, so that the Egyptians heard it, and the household of Pharaoh heard it.

45:3 And Yosef said to his brothers: I am Yosef. Is my father still alive? But his brothers could not answer him, for they were dismayed at his presence.

45:4 So Yosef said to his brothers: Come near to me, please. And they came near. And he said: I am your brother, Yosef, whom you sold into Egypt.

45:5 And now do not be distressed or angry with yourselves because you sold me here, for The Creator sent me before you to preserve life.

45:6 For the famine has been in the land these two years, and there are yet five years in which there will be neither plowing nor harvest.

45:7 And The Creator sent me before you to preserve for you a remnant on earth, and to keep alive for you many survivors.

45:8 So it was not you who sent me here, but The Creator. He has made me a father to Pharaoh, and lord of all his house and ruler over all the land of Egypt.

45:9 Hurry and go up to my father and say to him: Thus says your son Yosef, The Creator has made me lord of all Egypt. Come down to me — do not tarry.

45:10 You shall dwell in the land of Goshen, and you shall be near me, you and your children and your children's children, and your flocks, your herds, and all that you have.

45:11 There I will provide for you, for there are yet five years of famine to come, so that you and your household, and all that you have, do not come to poverty.

45:14 Then he fell upon his brother Binyamin's neck and wept, and Binyamin wept upon his neck.

45:15 And he kissed all his brothers and wept upon them. After that his brothers talked with him.

45:26 And they told him: Yosef is still alive, and he is ruler over all the land of Egypt. And his heart became numb, for he did not believe them.

45:27 But when they told him all the words of Yosef, which he had said to them, and when he saw the wagons that Yosef had sent to carry him, the spirit of their father Yaakov revived.

45:28 And Yisra'EL said: It is enough. Yosef my son is still alive. I will go and see him before I die.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena and 4QGenb preserve portions of Chapter 45. Yosef's self-revelation and the call to Yaakov are attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 45 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 45:5 — The Creator sent me before you to preserve life. And verse 45:8 — it was not you who sent me here, but The Creator. Yosef's theological interpretation of his own suffering is the climax of the entire narrative and one of the most profound theological statements in the Torah. He does not minimize what his brothers did — he sold me, verse 45:4. He does not excuse it. But he places it inside a larger purpose that only YHWH could see from the beginning. The pit, the slavery, the false accusation, the prison, the forgotten promise — each piece of the suffering was a step in YHWH's design to preserve life for the whole family and for the nations.

YAAKOV DESCENDS TO EGYPT — CHAPTERS 46 THROUGH 50

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 46 Yaakov Travels to Egypt — YHWH Speaks at Beer-Sheva — The Family List

46:1 So Yisra'EL took his journey with all that he had and came to Beer-Sheva, and offered sacrifices to The Creator of his father Yitzhak.

46:2 And The Creator spoke to Yisra'EL in visions of the night and said: Yaakov, Yaakov. And he said: Here I am.

46:3 Then he said: I am The Creator, The Creator of your father. Do not be afraid to go down to Egypt, for there I will make you into a great nation.

46:4 I myself will go down with you to Egypt, and I will also bring you up again, and Yosef's hand shall close your eyes.

46:5 Then Yaakov set out from Beer-Sheva. The sons of Yisra'EL carried Yaakov their father, their little ones, and their wives, in the wagons that Pharaoh had sent to carry him.

46:6 They also took their livestock and their goods, which they had gained in the land of Kena'an, and came into Egypt — Yaakov and all his offspring with him,

46:7 His sons, and his sons' sons with him, his daughters, and his sons' daughters. All his offspring he brought with him into Egypt.

46:8 Now these are the names of the descendants of Yisra'EL, who came into Egypt, Yaakov and his sons. Re'uven, Yaakov's firstborn.

46:9 And the sons of Re'uven: Hanokh, Pallu, Hetzron, and Karmi.

46:10 The sons of Shim'on: Yemu'el, Yamin, Ohad, Yakhin, Tzohar, and Sha'ul, the son of a Kena'anite woman.

46:11 The sons of Levi: Gershon, Kehat, and Merari.

46:12 The sons of Yehudah: Er, Onan, Shelah, Perets, and Zerah. Er and Onan died in the land of Kena'an. And the sons of Perets were Hetzron and Hamul.

46:13 The sons of Yissakhar: Tolah, Puvah, Yov, and Shimron.

46:14 The sons of Zevulun: Sered, Elon, and Yahle'el.

46:15 These are the sons of Leah, whom she bore to Yaakov in Paddan-Aram, together with his daughter Dinah — thirty-three persons in all.

46:16 The sons of Gad: Tzifyon, Haggi, Shuni, Etzbon, Eri, Arodi, and Areli.

46:17 The sons of Asher: Yimnah, Yishvah, Yishvi, Beriah, and their sister Serah. And the sons of Beriah: Hever and Malki'el.

46:18 These are the sons of Zilpah, whom Lavan gave to Leah his daughter — sixteen persons.

46:19 The sons of Rachel, Yaakov's wife: Yosef and Binyamin.

46:20 And to Yosef in the land of Egypt were born Manasseh and Efrayim, whom Asenath, the daughter of Poti-Fera the priest of On, bore to him.

46:21 And the sons of Binyamin: Bela, Bekher, Ashbel, Gera, Naaman, Ehi, Rosh, Muppim, Huppim, and Ard.

46:22 These are the sons of Rachel, who were born to Yaakov — fourteen persons in all.

46:23 The son of Dan: Hushim.

46:24 The sons of Naftali: Yahtze'el, Guni, Yetzer, and Shillem.

46:25 These are the sons of Bilhah, whom Lavan gave to Rachel his daughter — seven persons in all.

46:26 All the persons belonging to Yaakov who came into Egypt, who were his own descendants, not including Yaakov's sons' wives, were sixty-six persons in all.

46:27 And the sons of Yosef, who were born to him in Egypt, were two. All the persons of the house of Yaakov who came into Egypt were seventy.

46:28 He had sent Yehudah ahead of him to Yosef to show the way before him in Goshen, and they came into the land of Goshen.

46:29 Then Yosef prepared his chariot and went up to meet Yisra'EL his father in Goshen. He presented himself to him and fell on his neck and wept on his neck a good while.

46:30 Yisra'EL said to Yosef: Now let me die, since I have seen your face and know that you are still alive.

46:34 You shall say: Your servants have been keepers of livestock from our youth even until now, both we and our fathers — in order that you may dwell in the land of Goshen, for every shepherd is an abomination to the Egyptians.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena and 4QGenb preserve portions of Chapter 46. The descent of Yaakov into Egypt with the family list is attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 46 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The count of seventy persons in verse 46:27 is the Masoretic reading. The Septuagint and some other traditions give seventy-five. The DSS evidence for this specific verse is partial. The Masoretic seventy is followed here as the dominant ancient Hebrew reading and is consistent with the count as given in the text itself.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 46:3-4 — Do not be afraid to go down to Egypt, for there I will make you into a great nation. I myself will go down with you to Egypt, and I will also bring you up again. YHWH personally accompanies Yaakov into Egypt. This is the divine authorization for the descent — the event that leads to four hundred years of slavery and then the Exodus. YHWH goes down first. YHWH comes up last. Moshe's Exodus is already framed in YHWH's promise at Beer-Sheva before Yaakov takes a single step south.

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 47 Yaakov Before Pharaoh — The Famine — Yaakov's Years

47:1 So Yosef went in and told Pharaoh: My father and my brothers, with their flocks and herds and all that they possess, have come from the land of Kena'an. They are now in the land of Goshen.

47:2 And from among his brothers he took five men and presented them to Pharaoh.

47:3 Pharaoh said to his brothers: What is your occupation? And they said to Pharaoh: Your servants are shepherds, as our fathers were.

47:5 Then Pharaoh said to Yosef: Your father and your brothers have come to you.

47:6 The land of Egypt is before you. Settle your father and your brothers in the best of the land. Let them settle in the land of Goshen, and if you know any able men among them, put them in charge of my livestock.

47:7 Then Yosef brought in Yaakov his father and stood him before Pharaoh, and Yaakov blessed Pharaoh.

47:8 And Pharaoh said to Yaakov: How many are the days of the years of your life?

47:9 And Yaakov said to Pharaoh: The days of the years of my sojourning are 130 years. Few and evil have been the days of the years of my life, and they have not attained to the days of the years of the life of my fathers in the days of their sojourning.

47:10 And Yaakov blessed Pharaoh and went out from the presence of Pharaoh.

47:11 Then Yosef settled his father and his brothers and gave them a possession in the land of Egypt, in the best of the land, in the land of Ra'amses, as Pharaoh had commanded.

47:12 And Yosef provided his father, his brothers, and all his father's household with food, according to the number of their dependents.

47:13 Now there was no food in all the land, for the famine was very severe, so that the land of Egypt and the land of Kena'an languished by reason of the famine.

47:14 And Yosef gathered up all the money that was found in the land of Egypt and in the land of Kena'an, in exchange for the grain that they bought. And Yosef brought the money into Pharaoh's house.

47:27 Thus Yisra'EL settled in the land of Egypt, in the land of Goshen. And they gained possessions in it, and were fruitful and multiplied greatly.

47:28 And Yaakov lived in the land of Egypt seventeen years. So the days of Yaakov, the years of his life, were 147 years.

47:29 And when the time drew near that Yisra'EL must die, he called his son Yosef and said to him: If now I have found favor in your sight, put your hand under my thigh and promise to deal kindly and truly with me. Do not bury me in Egypt,

47:30 But let me lie with my fathers. Carry me out of Egypt and bury me in their burying place. He answered: I will do as you have said.

47:31 And he said: Swear to me. And he swore to him. Then Yisra'EL bowed himself upon the head of his bed.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena preserves portions of Chapter 47.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 47 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 47:9 — few and evil have been the days of the years of my life. Yaakov stands before the most powerful throne on earth and does not posture. He names his life honestly — few years, hard years. He is 130 years old and has known deception, exile, grief, the apparent death of his beloved son, and twenty years of mourning. The ancient record gives Yaakov this moment of blunt self-assessment. It is not despair. It is precision. He blessed Pharaoh before and after he said it.

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 48 Yaakov Blesses Manasseh and Efrayim — The Crossing of Hands

48:1 After this, Yosef was told: Your father is ill. So he took with him his two sons, Manasseh and Efrayim.

48:2 And it was told to Yaakov: Your son Yosef has come to you. Then Yisra'EL summoned his strength and sat up in bed.

48:3 And Yaakov said to Yosef: The Creator Almighty appeared to me at Luz in the land of Kena'an and blessed me,

48:4 And said to me: Behold, I will make you fruitful and multiply you, and I will make of you a company of peoples and will give this land to your offspring after you for an everlasting possession.

48:5 And now your two sons, who were born to you in the land of Egypt before I came to you in Egypt, are mine. Efrayim and Manasseh shall be mine, as Re'uven and Shim'on are.

48:6 And the children that you fathered after them shall be yours. They shall be called by the name of their brothers in their inheritance.

48:8 When Yisra'EL saw Yosef's sons, he said: Who are these?

48:9 Yosef said to his father: They are my sons, whom The Creator has given me here. And he said: Bring them to me, please, that I may bless them.

48:10 Now the eyes of Yisra'EL were dim with age, so that he could not see. Yosef brought them near him, and he kissed them and embraced them.

48:11 And Yisra'EL said to Yosef: I never expected to see your face — and behold, The Creator has let me see your offspring also.

48:13 And Yosef took them both — Efrayim in his right hand toward Yisra'EL's left hand, and Manasseh in his left hand toward Yisra'EL's right hand — and brought them near him.

48:14 And Yisra'EL stretched out his right hand and laid it on the head of Efrayim, who was the younger, and his left hand on the head of Manasseh, crossing his hands — for Manasseh was the firstborn.

48:15 And he blessed Yosef and said: The Creator before whom my fathers Avraham and Yitzhak walked, The Creator who has been my shepherd all my life long to this day,

48:16 The angel who has redeemed me from all evil, bless the boys — and in them let my name be carried on, and the name of my fathers Avraham and Yitzhak — and let them grow into a multitude in the midst of the earth.

48:17 When Yosef saw that his father laid his right hand on the head of Efrayim, it displeased him, and he took his father's hand to move it from Efrayim's head to Manasseh's head.

48:18 And Yosef said to his father: Not this way, my father — for this one is the firstborn. Put your right hand on his head.

48:19 But his father refused and said: I know, my son, I know. He also shall become a people, and he also shall be great. Nevertheless, his younger brother shall be greater than he, and his offspring shall become a multitude of nations.

48:20 So he blessed them that day, saying: By you Yisra'EL will pronounce blessings, saying: The Creator make you as Efrayim and as Manasseh. Thus he put Efrayim before Manasseh.

48:21 Then Yisra'EL said to Yosef: Behold, I am about to die, but The Creator will be with you and will bring you again to the land of your fathers.

48:22 Moreover, I have given to you rather than to your brothers one mountain slope that I took from the hand of the Amorites with my sword and with my bow.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena and 4QGenb preserve portions of Chapter 48. The blessing of Efrayim and Manasseh with the crossing of hands is attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 48 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 48:14 — Yisra'EL stretched out his right hand and laid it on the head of Efrayim, who was the younger, crossing his hands. The crossing of hands is deliberate. Yaakov knows exactly what he is doing — verse 48:19: I know, my son, I know. The reversal of birth order for covenant purposes runs throughout all of Bereshit — Yitzhak over Yishma'el, Yaakov over Esav, Yosef over Re'uven, Efrayim over Manasseh. It is never an accident. It is never because the elder was unworthy. It is YHWH's sovereign choice, recognized and enacted by the patriarch.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 48:16 — the angel who has redeemed me from all evil, bless the boys. Yaakov invokes three: The Creator who walked with Avraham and Yitzhak, The Creator who shepherded him all his life, and the angel who redeemed him from all evil. The angel who redeemed is the angel of YHWH — the same figure as the Angel of Presence in Yovelim and the wrestler at Peni'el. Yaakov gives the angel equal standing in his deathbed blessing. This is consistent with the DSS theology of the Angel of Presence as YHWH's direct representative with full divine authority.

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 49 The Blessing of the Twelve Tribes — Yaakov's Last Words

49:1 Then Yaakov called his sons and said: Gather yourselves together, that I may tell you what shall happen to you in days to come.

49:2 Assemble and listen, O sons of Yaakov — listen to Yisra'EL your father.

49:3 Re'uven, you are my firstborn, my might, and the firstfruits of my strength, preeminent in dignity and preeminent in power.

49:4 Unstable as water, you shall not have preeminence, because you went up to your father's bed — then you defiled it. He went up to my couch.

49:5 Shim'on and Levi are brothers — weapons of violence are their swords.

49:6 Let my soul come not into their council — O my glory, be not joined to their company. For in their anger they killed men, and in their willfulness they hamstrung oxen.

49:7 Cursed be their anger, for it is fierce, and their wrath, for it is cruel. I will divide them in Yaakov and scatter them in Yisra'EL.

49:8 Yehudah, your brothers shall praise you — your hand shall be on the neck of your enemies — your father's sons shall bow down before you.

49:9 Yehudah is a lion's cub — from the prey, my son, you have gone up. He crouched and lay down like a lion — and like a lioness, who dares rouse him?

49:10 The scepter shall not depart from Yehudah, nor the ruler's staff from between his feet, until tribute comes to him — and to him shall be the obedience of the peoples.

49:11 Binding his foal to the vine and his donkey's colt to the choice vine, he has washed his garments in wine and his vesture in the blood of grapes.

49:12 His eyes are darker than wine, and his teeth whiter than milk.

49:13 Zevulun shall dwell at the shore of the sea — he shall become a haven for ships, and his border shall be at Tzidon.

49:14 Yissakhar is a strong donkey, crouching between the sheepfolds.

49:15 He saw that a resting place was good, and that the land was pleasant, so he bowed his shoulder to bear, and became a servant at forced labor.

49:16 Dan shall judge his people as one of the tribes of Yisra'EL.

49:17 Dan shall be a serpent in the way, a viper by the path, that bites the horse's heels so that his rider falls backward.

49:18 I wait for your salvation, O YHWH.

49:19 Raiders shall raid Gad, but he shall raid at their heels.

49:20 Asher's food shall be rich, and he shall yield royal delicacies.

49:21 Naftali is a doe let loose that bears beautiful fawns.

49:22 Yosef is a fruitful bough, a fruitful bough by a spring — his branches run over the wall.

49:23 The archers bitterly attacked him, shot at him, and harassed him severely,

49:24 Yet his bow remained unmoved — his arms were made agile by the hands of the Mighty One of Yaakov — from there is the Shepherd, the Stone of Yisra'EL —

49:25 By The Creator of your father who will help you, by The Creator Almighty who will bless you with blessings of heaven above, blessings of the deep that crouches beneath, blessings of the breasts and of the womb.

49:26 The blessings of your father are mighty beyond the blessings of my parents, up to the bounties of the everlasting hills. May they be on the head of Yosef, and on the brow of him who was set apart from his brothers.

49:27 Binyamin is a ravenous wolf — in the morning devouring the prey and at evening dividing the spoil.

49:28 All these are the twelve tribes of Yisra'EL. This is what their father said to them as he blessed them, blessing each with the blessing suitable to him.

49:29 Then he commanded them and said to them: I am to be gathered to my people — bury me with my fathers in the cave that is in the field of Efron the Hittite,

49:30 In the cave that is in the field at Makhpelah, to the east of Mamre, in the land of Kena'an, which Avraham bought with the field from Efron the Hittite to possess as a burying place.

49:31 There they buried Avraham and Sarah his wife. There they buried Yitzhak and Rivkah his wife, and there I buried Leah —

49:32 The field and the cave that is in it were bought from the Hittites.

49:33 When Yaakov finished commanding his sons, he drew up his feet into the bed and breathed his last and was gathered to his people.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena, 4QGenb, and 4QGenj preserve portions of Chapter 49. The tribal blessings are among the most attested passages in the Cave 4 Genesis manuscripts. The blessing of Yehudah — verse 49:10 — is particularly well preserved.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 49 in full. The tribal blessings are consistent with the DSS text with only minor orthographic differences.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in the core blessing text of this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 49:4 — Re'uven, unstable as water, you shall not have preeminence because you went up to your father's bed. The deed of Chapter 35:22 receives its reckoning here at the end of Yaakov's life. Re'uven loses the firstborn's double portion — it passes to Yosef through the tribes of Efrayim and Manasseh. The firstborn title by honor goes to Yehudah. The ancient record holds the account open from Chapter 35 to Chapter 49 — fourteen chapters apart. Nothing in the Torah is without consequence.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 49:10 — the scepter shall not depart from Yehudah, nor the ruler's staff from between his feet, until tribute comes to him. This is the foundational dynastic promise to Yehudah in the Torah — the covenant of kingship rooted in Bereshit before any king ever ruled Yisra'EL. Dawid's throne is the fulfillment of Yaakov's deathbed word over Yehudah. The ancient Hebrew kingdom is not a political accident — it is a covenant promise spoken over a deathbed in Egypt.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 49:18 — I wait for your salvation, O YHWH. This single verse — spoken in the middle of the tribal blessings — is Yaakov's personal cry inserted into the prophetic poetry. He is speaking over Dan and pauses to address YHWH directly. The ancient record preserves this interruption as it occurred. The patriarch is not reciting a prepared text. He is speaking what comes — prophecy and prayer intertwined.

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 50 The Burial of Yaakov — Yosef's Final Years — The Death of Yosef

50:1 Then Yosef fell on his father's face and wept over him and kissed him.

50:2 And Yosef commanded his servants the physicians to embalm his father. So the physicians embalmed Yisra'EL.

50:3 Forty days were required for it, for that is how many are required for embalming. And the Egyptians wept for him seventy days.

50:4 And when the days of weeping for him were past, Yosef spoke to the household of Pharaoh, saying: If now I have found favor in your eyes, please speak in the ears of Pharaoh, saying,

50:5 My father made me swear, saying: I am about to die. In my tomb that I hewed out for myself in the land of Kena'an, there shall you bury me. Now therefore, let me please go up and bury my father. Then I will return.

50:7 So Yosef went up to bury his father. With him went up all the servants of Pharaoh, the elders of his household, and all the elders of the land of Egypt,

50:8 As well as all the household of Yosef, his brothers, and his father's household. Only their children, their flocks, and their herds were left in the land of Goshen.

50:9 And there went up with him both chariots and horsemen. It was a very great company.

50:12 Thus his sons did for him as he had commanded them,

50:13 For his sons carried him to the land of Kena'an and buried him in the cave of the field at Makhpelah, to the east of Mamre, which Avraham bought with the field from Efron the Hittite to possess as a burying place.

50:14 After he had buried his father, Yosef returned to Egypt with his brothers and all who had gone up with him to bury his father.

50:15 When Yosef's brothers saw that their father was dead, they said: It may be that Yosef will hate us and pay us back for all the evil that we did to him.

50:16 So they sent a message to Yosef, saying: Your father gave this command before he died:

50:17 Say to Yosef: Please forgive the transgression of your brothers and their sin, because they did evil to you. And now, please forgive the transgression of the servants of The Creator of your father. Yosef wept when they spoke to him.

50:18 His brothers also came and fell down before him and said: Behold, we are your servants.

50:19 But Yosef said to them: Do not fear, for am I in the place of The Creator?

50:20 As for you, you intended evil against me — but The Creator intended it for good, to bring it about that many people should be kept alive, as they are today.

50:21 So do not fear — I will provide for you and your little ones. Thus he comforted them and spoke kindly to them.

50:22 So Yosef lived in Egypt, he and his father's house. Yosef lived 110 years.

50:23 And Yosef saw Efrayim's children of the third generation. The children also of Makhir the son of Manasseh were counted as Yosef's own.

50:24 And Yosef said to his brothers: I am about to die, but The Creator will visit you and bring you up out of this land to the land that he swore to Avraham, to Yitzhak, and to Yaakov.

50:25 Then Yosef made the sons of Yisra'EL swear, saying: The Creator will surely visit you, and you shall carry up my bones from here.

50:26 So Yosef died, being 110 years old. They embalmed him, and he was put in a coffin in Egypt.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena, 4QGenb, and 4QGenk preserve portions of Chapter 50. The burial of Yaakov at Makhpelah and the final words of Yosef are attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 50 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 50:19-20 — do not fear, for am I in the place of The Creator? As for you, you intended evil against me — but The Creator intended it for good. This is the theological apex of the entire Book of Bereshit. Yosef holds both truths without collapsing one into the other. The brothers intended evil — that is not erased. The Creator intended good — that does not excuse the evil. Both are true simultaneously. YHWH works through human wickedness without endorsing it and without being limited by it. The coffin in Egypt at verse 50:26 is not a defeat — Yosef himself has already declared the outcome in verse 50:24: The Creator will surely visit you and bring you up out of this land.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 50:25 — you shall carry up my bones from here. Yosef's final act is to bind the next generation to the covenant land through a sworn oath over his bones. He will not be buried in Kena'an at death — he will be carried there at the Exodus. Shemot 13:19 records that Moshe took the bones of Yosef when Yisra'EL left Egypt. Yehoshua 24:32 records their final burial at Shechem. A deathbed oath made in Egypt reaches its fulfillment forty years and two books later. The Torah holds its threads across the entire narrative. Yosef's bones travel the Exodus with the people.

END OF BERESHIT — VOLUME ONE COMPLETE
